lines.en_lit: in the future, will bubble from under the threshold of the Temple. From this forward, will grow in strength and rise until it reaches the opening of David’s house, When it reaches the opening of David’s house, it will become a flowing river in which zavim and zavot, menstruating women, and women after childbirth will bathe As it is stated: “On that day there shall be a fountain opened for the house of David and to the inhabitants of Jerusalem for purification and for sprinkling”
Rav Yosef said: From here, there is an allusion that a menstruating woman must sit up to her neck in water, But the halakha is not in accordance with his

well Yom Kippur there are no shoes, Shabbat, when there are shoes, what Neḥemya, the son-in-law of the Nasi, said: I saw Rabbi Ami and Rabbi Asi come to a pool of water and they crossed it while wearing
well shoes what is there to say sandals? Rav Riḥumi said: I saw Ravina cross wearing Rav Ashi said: If he is sandals, not ab initio,

The Exilarch came to Rav Natan’s study hall in Hagronya. Rafram and all the Sages came to the lecture, Ravina did not come. The next day, Rafram wanted to remove Ravina from the mind the Exilarch, What is the reason the Master did not come to the lecture? He said to him: My foot hurt. You should have put shoes on. It was the back of the foot

You should have worn sandals, He said to him: There was a pool of water on the way He said to him: You should have crossed it wearing He said to him: Does the Master not hold Rav Ashi said: not sandals ab initio?

Yehuda bar Gerogarot taught: It is prohibited to sit on clay on Yom Kippur. Rabbi Yehoshua ben Levi said: clay dripping wet, Abaye said dripping wet enough to make something else wet. Rav Yehuda said: permitted to cool off with fruit Rav Yehuda cooled off a squash

Rabba cooled off by a baby [yanuka] Rava cooled off with a silver cup. Rav Pappa said: silver cup full, it is prohibited; not full, it is permitted. a ceramic this and that are prohibited, since seeps through Rav said: A silver cup that is not full is also prohibited because it can slip [mizderiv]

Ze’eira bar Ḥama, host Rabbi Ami, and Rabbi Asi, and Rabbi Yehoshua ben Levi, and all the Sages of Caesarea said to Rav Yosef, son of Rabbi Yehoshua ben Levi: Son of a lion, come, I will tell you a wonderful custom that your father used to perform. He had a cloth that on the day before Yom Kippur by soaking it in water and making it like a dried cloth. And the next day he wiped his face, hands, and feet with it. the day before the Ninth of Av, soak the in water and the next day pass it over his eyes.

Similarly, when Rabba bar Mari came he said: The day before the Ninth of Av they bring one a cloth, and he soak it in water and place it under his head. The next day, he wipe his face, hands, and feet On the day before Yom Kippur, they bring one a cloth, and he soak it in water and make it like a dried cloth. The next day, he pass it over his eyes. Rabbi Ya’akov said to Rabbi Yirmeya bar Taḥlifa: You told us the opposite. and we objected to you wringing,

Rav Menashya bar Taḥalifa said Rav Amram said Rabba bar bar Ḥana said: They asked Rabbi Elazar an Elder who sits in a yeshiva receive permission to permit firstborn animals permitted, to firstborn animals permitted, or not?

What are they asking? This is what they are asking, like this Rav Idi bar Avin, said: This matter, was given to the house of the Nasi to raise its stature. must permission be received, Or, perhaps because is an Elder who sits in a yeshiva, there is no need. Rav Tzadok ben Ḥaluka rose to his feet and said: I saw Rabbi Yosei ben Zimra, who was an Elder who sat in the yeshiva and who stood before the grandfather of this ask permission to permit firstborn animals.

Rabbi Abba said to him: was not how the incident was. Rather this was the incident: Rabbi Yosei ben Zimra was a priest, and he raised the following dilemma: Is the halakha in accordance with Rabbi Meir, said: One who is suspect in area not judge it and not testify it? Or perhaps the halakha is in accordance with Rabban Shimon ben Gamliel, said: is believed about his fellow but is not believed about himself. He resolved him: halakha is in accordance with Rabban Shimon ben Gamliel.
They raised dilemma them, What is going out in sandals made of
